summary: Comprehensive coverage of core requirements: all three version bump types (major/minor/patch) tested via both CLI integration and e2e workflows; version file parsing for JSON and plain text; changelog generation with grouping and scope formatting; conventional commit parsing with breaking changes, scopes, and type detection. Workflow validation with actionlint integration is strong. Significant gaps: missing error scenarios (invalid JSON in package.json, missing commits.txt file), no boundary conditions (version 0.0.0, integer overflow), no idempotency testing (appending to existing CHANGELOG on multiple runs), limited edge cases (single breaking change, complex multiline bodies). Test design is clean with good separation of concerns (unit/integration/e2e/structure tests), proper cleanup, and clear naming, but fixture organization could be stronger (fixtures referenced but not shown, no validation of their content). Would catch regressions in happy path and common errors but miss unusual input combinations and edge cases.
